objective: Close F-0118 by replacing the fixed five-row current-node employee preview with a read-only organization-admin roster whose scope is derived from the bound organization and valid descendant tree, whose search/count/page use one repository predicate, and whose rows expose only employee public ids, masked phones, account lock/disable state and the existing effective-authorization summary. Preserve the platform-owned mutation boundary and fail closed on out-of-scope public ids without schema, migration, persistent database execution or new entitlement semantics.
blockedFiles: schema, migration or persistent database execution
organization tree mutation or employee mutation
new role, permission, entitlement, quota or default capability
plaintext phone disclosure or phone-fragment search
Provider, external service, secret/env, deployment, PR or force-push

authoritativeChainEvidence.uiApi: AdminOrganizationPortalPage renders overview.employees as a complete employee roster although the overview repository returns only five current-node rows. A dedicated GET /api/v1/organization-portal-employees collection will own name search, account/auth filters, page/pageSize and employeePublicId support lookup while the overview remains summary-only.
authoritativeChainEvidence.routeService: The existing organization-portal session resolver already restricts access to org_standard_admin and org_advanced_admin and supplies the bound organization public id. The new read-only handler reuses that resolver, normalizes 20/50/100 pagination and returns no-store responses without adding a mutation route.
authoritativeChainEvidence.repository: The roster repository will use one bound-organization self-or-descendant predicate for rows and count, stable display-name plus employee-public-id ordering, masked phone mapping, explicit locked/disabled precedence, and the F-0107 effective-authorization read model. Optional employeePublicId lookup remains inside the same scope predicate so out-of-tree ids fail closed as an empty result.
authoritativeChainEvidence.schema: Existing organization parent links, employee.public_id, unique employee.user_id, user.locked_until_at, employee_org_auth, org_auth and auth_upgrade facts are sufficient. No schema/migration source or persistent database execution is required.
authoritativeChainEvidence.tests: Focused RED covers the 6th/21st/101st employees, descendant inclusion and sibling exclusion, identical row/count predicates, 20/50/100 pagination, stable ordering, URL-restored filters, public-id scoped lookup, masked phones, disabled-over-locked precedence, expired/active authorization summaries and no write controls. Direct route/UI/F-0119/F-0107/tree-scope regressions are pre-allowlisted.
authoritativeChainEvidence.requirements: The 2026-07-02 role/auth decision package D04/D09 and 2026-07-07 organization-admin workspace baseline require an organization-scoped read-only roster/status surface while all employee and tree mutation stays platform-owned. ADR-007 and edition-aware authorization requirements keep the effective authorization source and edition semantics unchanged.
authoritativeChainEvidence.nonClaims: No new role, entitlement or organization scope beyond the documented bound organization and valid descendants; no employee detail mutation, full phone, raw authorization rows, raw answers, schema/migration, real database access, dependency, Provider, external service, secret/env, deployment, PR, force-push or mechanism change.
